title: uordlst
text: uordlst Describes the requested ordering of selected records. Description An array of uordlst structures (informally called the order-by list), is used to describe the requested ordering of selected records. Uniface supports multifield ordering—if two records are equal with respect to the first entry in the order-by list, they are ordered according to the second entry and so on. If two records are identical in all fields in the order-by list, their order is undefined. If the DBMS is not capable of selecting records in the requested order, Uniface can handle the sorting. The uordlst structure is also used to specify the computation to be performed in the Select mode 4 request. For more information, see the "Database Connector Requests topic". The connector can access the uordlst structure by using the address of the first element provided in the current uctrl structure. The order-by list is available in: ControlBlock ->uolist[0]... ControlBlock ->uolist[( ControlBlock ->uonum -1)] where ControlBlock contains the address of the current uctrl structure. Members Table 1. uordlst Members Member Description short ufnr Field number (an index into the field list). short uorder Ordering code; one of: 0—Ascending order. 1—Descending order. Related Links Database Connector Requests A database connector implements a set of generic Uniface requests for the underlying DBMS. Connector requests are well-defined tasks performed by the DBMS connector when it is called. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
